sutta:
on a certain occasion the exalted 1 was staying at saketa in kalakaspa then the exalted 1 addressed the monk saying monks yes lord replied those monks to the exalted 1 the exalted 1 said monks whatsoever in the world with its devas etc etc with its hopes of recluses and brahmins of devas and mankind whatsoever is seen heard sensed cognized attained searched into pondered over the by the mind all that do i know whatsoever is seen sensed cognized attained searched into bonded over by the mind then have i fully comprehended all that is understood by the tathagata but the tatragata is not subject to it if i were to say i know whatsoever in the world is seen heard and so forth it would be a falsehood in me if i were to say i both know it and know it not it would be a falsehood in me if i were to say i neither know it nor am ignorant of it it would be a falsehood in me that would be a fault in me thus monks the tagatha is a seer of 1 is to be seen but he has no conceit of what is seen he has no conceit of what has not been seen he has no conceit of what is to be seen he has no conceit about the seer hearing 1 is to be heard he has no conceit of what has been heard or not heard or is to be heard he has no conceit about the hearer so also sensing 1 is to be sensed cognizing the cognizable he has no conceit of the thing cognized or to be cognized of him who has cognition thus monks the tagatha being such a 1 in things seen heard sensed cognized is such moreover then he who is such there is none other greater or more excellent i declare this the end of the suita this 1 of the sutasana which is a bit deep and it's not easily understood because the state of a buddha of an enlightened being we we cannot understand but basically what the buddha is trying to say is even though he is a seer of 1 is to be seen he has no conceit of what is seen he has no conceit of what has not been seen he has no conceit of what is to be seen he has no conceit about the seer so basically here what he's trying to say is there is no um no self luck even though they seen there is just the same there is no i see uh there is no that ego behind so he has destroyed that ego so he he looks at um everything a bit different from us we have still have got the ask of us the mental outflows whenever we see something through our senses we recognize through our 6 senses at the moment um our consciousness arises immediately it's followed by feeling uh whether it's a pleasant feeling or unpleasant feeling or neutral 1 and after that there's perception there's a perception and and an idea about that that thing like like you hear a sound and then uh then you perceive on this this this follows the mind starts working you start thinking and then anger rises and then volition will follow you decide what to do or what not to do and then and then this will repeat and the process and the you keep on thinking about it and this uh results in papancha it's a proliferation of thoughts that 1 thought becomes 10 10 becomes 100 etc but with the buddha no thoughts arise it's destroyed the earth of us whatever is seen it just sees it the way it is very naturally and with our eyes seeing without any proliferation of thoughts that's the state of an arahant so it's quite difficult for us to understand it's basically that whatever happens and there is no i because the eye arises with the thinking that's why thinking is the support system of our ego that's why in meditation now we try to stop all the thinking and you can't stop the thinking until you have still your mind made your mind so still that all the thoughts have stopped now then you're used to that statement slowly the mind inclines towards that state and then uh otherwise now we are used to thinking we always we always live in the world of the 6 senses it's very hard to withdraw from the world of the 6 senses
